sections.content: These 50 words appear again and again across every traditional prayer, the Mass, and the Divine Office. Learning them is the single fastest way to begin understanding Latin at Mass. You do not need to memorize them all at once — revisit this list regularly and they will become second nature.

sections.title: God and the Trinity
sections.content:
• Deus — God
• Dóminus — Lord
• Christus — Christ
• Iesus — Jesus
• Pater — Father
• Fílius — Son
• Spíritus Sanctus — Holy Ghost/Spirit
• omnípotens — almighty
• ætérnus — eternal

sections.title: Our Lady and the Saints
sections.content:
• María — Mary
• Virgo — Virgin
• Mater — Mother
• Regína — Queen
• beátus/beáta — blessed
• Sanctus/Sancta — holy, saint
• Ángelus — Angel
• Apóstolus — Apostle

sections.title: Prayer and Worship
sections.content:
• oráre — to pray
• laudáre — to praise
• adoráre — to adore
• benedícere — to bless
• credo — I believe
• grátia — grace
• glória — glory
• misericórdia — mercy
• pax — peace
• amen — so be it

sections.title: Sin, Salvation, and the Spiritual Life
sections.content:
• peccátum — sin
• peccátor — sinner
• salus — salvation
• redemptio — redemption
• remíssio — forgiveness
• mors — death
• vita — life
• lux — light
• veritas — truth
• fides — faith

sections.title: Essential Small Words
sections.content:
These tiny words appear dozens of times in every Mass:

• et — and
• in — in
• cum — with
• pro — for
• ad — to, toward
• per — through
• qui — who
• quia — because
• sed — but
• nunc — now
• semper — always
• nobis — to us, for us
• tuus/tua — your, thy
• noster/nostra — our
• meus/mea — my

sections.title: Mass-Specific Words
sections.content:
• Missa — Mass
• altáre — altar
• sacrifícium — sacrifice
• hóstia — host (victim/offering)
• cæli/cælum — heaven
• terra — earth
• corpus — body
• sanguis — blood
• panis — bread
• verbum — word

items.note: et = and, cum = with, spíritu = spirit, tuo = thy

items.meaning: Glory to the Father and to the Son
items.note: Glória = glory, Patri = to the Father, Fílio = to the Son

items.latin: Agnus Dei qui tollis peccáta mundi
items.meaning: Lamb of God who takes away the sins of the world
items.note: Agnus = Lamb, Dei = of God, peccáta = sins, mundi = of the world
Dona = grant, nobis = to us, pacem = peace

items.latin: Sancta María, Mater Dei, ora pro nobis
items.meaning: Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us
items.note: Every word from our vocabulary list

sections.content: These 50 words cover roughly 80% of the vocabulary in the prayers and the Mass Ordinary. You do not need to learn them all at once. Start with the Mass-Specific and Essential Small Words — these are the ones you will hear every Sunday. The rest will come naturally with repeated exposure.
